tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Report what this session controls: the master account/subaccount, whether it can trade or is read-only, and the exact on-chain authorization scope (which messages, which subaccount, and that it CANNOT withdraw/transfer). Call this first.

tools.name: get_block_height
tools.description: Return the latest chain block height. Useful for SHORT_TERM order goodTilBlock math.

tools.name: list_markets
tools.description: List all perpetual markets with ticker, status and oracle price. Use this first to discover valid market tickers (e.g. BTC-USD) before placing orders.

tools.name: get_market
tools.description: Get details for one perpetual market (clobPairId, atomicResolution, quantumConversionExponent, tick/step sizes, oracle price, status).

tools.name: get_orderbook
tools.description: Get the live orderbook (bids/asks) for a market, optionally truncated to a depth.

tools.name: get_candles
tools.description: Get OHLCV candles for a market at a given resolution. Useful for trend/volatility analysis.

tools.name: get_candles_multi
tools.description: Get recent OHLCV candles for a market across MULTIPLE resolutions in one call — by default ALL indexer resolutions (1MIN, 5MINS, 15MINS, 30MINS, 1HOUR, 4HOURS, 1DAY) — so the agent can read the trend across timeframes at once. Returns { candles: { <resolution>: [...] } }, newest first. Lower `limit`

tools.name: get_balance
tools.description: Get the subaccount collateral (USDC asset position) and the on-chain wallet bank balances (gas). Collateral is what backs trading.

tools.name: get_equity
tools.description: Get the subaccount summary: equity, freeCollateral, marginEnabled, open perpetual positions and asset positions.

tools.name: get_positions
tools.description: Get perpetual POSITIONS with size, side, entryPrice, unrealizedPnl, realizedPnl and netFunding. Defaults to OPEN positions. NOTE: a position is what you HOLD after an order fills — it is NOT an open order. When the user asks loosely "what is open?", check BOTH this and get_open_orders (or call get_p

tools.name: get_open_orders
tools.description: Get currently active ORDERS for the subaccount, optionally filtered by market and side. Includes OPEN resting orders and UNTRIGGERED conditional orders (TP/SL accepted on-chain but not yet triggered). Returns { orders: [...] }. This does NOT include filled positions — a filled market/limit order lea

tools.name: get_portfolio
tools.description: One-call snapshot of everything the account has "open": equity & freeCollateral, every open POSITION (with notional, unrealized PnL and estimated liquidation price), every active ORDER (resting + untriggered TP/SL), and account margin risk. Use this to answer "what do I have open?" or "how am I doin

tools.name: get_position_risk
tools.description: Margin health for the subaccount: equity, freeCollateral, total maintenance/initial margin, maintenanceMarginBufferUsd (the EXACT liquidation guard — liquidatable when < 0), marginUsageRatio (1.0 = at liquidation), account leverage, and per-position estimated liquidation price + distance to it. Use

tools.name: get_fills
tools.description: Get executed fills for the subaccount, including price, size, fee and maker/taker liquidity.

tools.name: get_pnl
tools.description: Get historical PnL ticks for the subaccount (equity, totalPnl, netTransfers over time). Use createdOnOrAfter to bound the range.

tools.name: place_limit_order
tools.description: Place a limit order. Default timeInForce=GTT (long-term, durable, broadcast-commit so errors return synchronously). Use SHORT_TERM only for latency-sensitive orders (expire within ~20 blocks, broadcast async). NOTE: stateful (GTT) orders are rate-limited to 2/block and 20 per 100 blocks — pace place

tools.name: place_market_order
tools.description: Place an IOC market order. It executes as an IOC limit at a worst-acceptable price mirrored around the oracle by side — BUY caps ABOVE the oracle, SELL below — at slippageBps distance (default 500 = 5%). Fills happen at book prices; the cap only limits how deep the sweep goes. reduceOnly closes an e

tools.name: cancel_order
tools.description: Cancel a single order by clientId. For SHORT_TERM pass goodTilBlock; for LONG_TERM/CONDITIONAL pass goodTilTimeSeconds (the original good-til value, available from get_open_orders). Returns `confirmation` with the verified result (canceled / still_open / filled / pending): `code: 0` only means the c

tools.name: cancel_all_orders
tools.description: Cancel every OPEN order for the subaccount in a market (handles SHORT_TERM and LONG_TERM automatically). Returns `confirmation.remainingOpen` — orders still OPEN after the cancel (0 = all gone); if > 0, retry.

tools.name: batch_cancel
tools.description: Cancel multiple SHORT_TERM orders in one tx. SHORT_TERM only (chain limitation) — for stateful/mixed use cancel_all_orders.

tools.name: place_stop_loss
tools.description: Place a reduce-only Stop loss (conditional market order) that triggers at triggerPrice. Side must be the CLOSING side of the position: SELL closes a LONG, BUY closes a SHORT. Once triggered it executes as an IOC limit bounded at triggerPrice ± slippageBps in the direction of execution (BUY above the

tools.name: place_take_profit
tools.description: Place a reduce-only Take profit (conditional market order) that triggers at triggerPrice. Side must be the CLOSING side of the position: SELL closes a LONG, BUY closes a SHORT. Once triggered it executes as an IOC limit bounded at triggerPrice ± slippageBps in the direction of execution (BUY above t

tools.name: close_position
tools.description: Close all or part of an open perpetual position with a reduce-only MARKET order. Reads the current position, flips the side automatically (LONG→SELL, SHORT→BUY) and sizes the close. percent defaults to 100 (full close); a partial close is floored to the market step size. The close is bounded at orac

tools.name: close_all_positions
tools.description: Flatten EVERY open perpetual position with reduce-only MARKET orders (one per market). Respects the operator market allowlist (disallowed markets are skipped and reported). Each close is bounded at oracle ± slippageBps mirrored by side (default 500 bps = 5%). Returns a per-market result array — for

tools.name: open_position
tools.description: Enter a NEW position with a MARKET order, sized by base `size` OR quote `notionalUsd` (exactly one required; notionalUsd converts at the oracle price and is floored to the step size). Optionally attach a reduce-only stopLossPrice and/or takeProfitPrice as conditional orders in the SAME call (the bra

tools.name: get_news
tools.description: Fetch the latest headlines from the ritbit news feed (curated crypto / markets / business channels, newest first). Filter by `category` (crypto|markets|business) or explicit `channels`, and/or a free-text `query` (a ticker or keyword, matched in the post text and hashtags, e.g. BTC). Each item retur

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "whoami" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Report what this session controls: the master account/subaccount, whether it can trade or is read-only, and the exact on-chain authorization scope (which messag…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: whoami, get_news · Private data: get_balance · Public sink: place_limit_order, place_market_order, cancel_order, open_position, get_news · Destructive: close_position, close_all_positions.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: whoami, get_news · Private data: get_balance · Public sink: place_limit_order, place_market_order, cancel_order, open_position, get_news · Destructive: close_position, close_all_positions

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
